cases.title: registration-status returns null when participant has no registration for event
preconditions: Event exists in RegistrationOpen state
Authenticated participant has no active or historical registration row for the event
steps: Invoke registrationService.getStatus(eventId, participantId) for the participant
Inspect returned registration field
cases.expected: Response contains registration null; participant can query status without error and sees unregistered state; baseline for FR-10 tracking before any registration action

cases.title: GET registration-status reflects Registered immediately after successful registration
preconditions: Published event with registration opened and available seats
Authenticated participant with no prior registration
Fresh Idempotency-Key for POST registrations
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Authorization bearer token
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status for the same participant
cases.expected: POST returns state Registered; GET returns HTTP 200 with registration object whose state is Registered, registrationId matches POST response, and updatedAt is present; participant can track confirmed seat status in near real-time

cases.title: GET registration-status reflects Waitlisted with queue position when event is full
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Second participant authenticated with no active registration
Fresh Idempotency-Key header
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations as the waitlisted participant
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status for the same participant
cases.expected: GET returns registration with state Waitlisted and waitlistPosition reflecting queue order; status matches POST response body; participant can track waitlist position without organizer intervention

cases.title: registration-status updates from Waitlisted to Registered after waitlist promotion
preconditions: Participant A is Registered and participant B is Waitlisted on a full event
Participant A completes valid cancellation releasing one seat
Waitlist promotion executes atomically per registration module contract
steps: GET registration-status for participant B before cancellation
Trigger cancellation and waitlist promotion for participant B
GET registration-status for participant B after promotion completes
cases.expected: Initial status shows Waitlisted with queue position; subsequent GET shows state Registered with waitlistPosition cleared or null; participant B tracks promotion outcome without re-registering per Flow 2 step 5

cases.title: registration-status reflects CheckedIn after valid staff check-in
preconditions: Participant has Registered state for an InProgress event
Check-in window is open per BR-10
Staff actor authorized for the event
steps: POST check-in for the participant registration within the valid window
GET registration-status for the participant
cases.expected: registration-status returns state CheckedIn (or equivalent post-check-in state per state machine); status reflects check-in write and registration state transition in one transaction

cases.title: registration-status reflects Attended or Absent after event completion
preconditions: Event transitions to Completed
Participant had Registered registration with or without valid check-in
steps: Complete event lifecycle to Completed triggering attendance finalization
GET registration-status for participant with valid check-in
GET registration-status for participant without check-in
cases.expected: Participant with valid check-in sees Attended; participant without check-in sees Absent; latest registration snapshot is returned (not filtered out as inactive) so post-event tracking remains available

cases.title: GET me/registrations returns paginated list of participant registrations with current states
preconditions: Authenticated participant registered for at least two distinct events in different states
Events span Registered and Waitlisted outcomes
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with Authorization bearer token
Compare each item state to GET registration-status for the corresponding eventId
cases.expected: Paginated envelope returns items with registrationId, eventId, eventName, state, and updatedAt; each item state matches per-event registration-status; single API call per page with no N+1 fan-out per testing plan AC-14

cases.title: Event detail page registration status panel reflects outcome after register action
preconditions: Participant logged in on event detail page for RegistrationOpen event with available seats
No existing registration for the event
steps: Click Register on event detail page
Observe registration status panel or badge after request completes
cases.expected: UI displays Registered status with positive confirmation; panel updates without full page reload; aligns with user Flow 1 steps 3–5 and wireframe registration status panel

cases.title: Event discovery cards surface per-event registration status for authenticated participant
preconditions: Participant logged in viewing paginated event discovery list
Participant is Registered for at least one listed event and not registered for another
steps: Navigate to event discovery page
Inspect registration status badge on registered event card
Inspect registration status badge on unregistered event card
cases.expected: Registered event card shows Registered (or current state) instead of Not registered; unregistered card shows Not registered or equivalent neutral state; per-event status fetched for visible cards

cases.title: My Registrations page reflects latest status after registration outcome
preconditions: Participant completes registration on an event yielding Registered or Waitlisted
My Registrations route is available to authenticated participants
steps: Complete registration via event detail flow
Navigate to My Registrations page
Locate the event row and read displayed status
cases.expected: My Registrations lists the event with state matching registration-status API; user Flow 1 step 6 satisfied — latest status visible in consolidated participant view

cases.title: Unauthenticated registration-status request returns 401
preconditions: Valid eventId exists
No Authorization header or expired JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status without bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with typed error envelope; registration data not exposed; auth required before sensitive participant data per NFR-07 and AC-15

cases.title: registration-status returns only the current actor registration not other participants
preconditions: Two distinct participants A and B registered for the same event
Participant A authenticated context
steps: GET registration-status as participant A
GET registration-status as participant B
Attempt to pass participant B id in query or body if supported
cases.expected: Each call returns only the requesting participant's registration snapshot; participantId in response matches token subject; FR-26 own-data scope enforced

cases.title: registration-status reflects CancelledByUser after valid participant cancellation
preconditions: Participant Registered for event before CancellationDeadline
Cancellation policy allows participant-initiated cancel
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel with valid Idempotency-Key
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status
cases.expected: GET returns registration with cancelled state (CancelledByUser or equivalent terminal cancel state); participant tracks cancellation outcome immediately after successful cancel

cases.title: registration-status reflects Rejected when registration rejected on full event without waitlist
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen at capacity with waitlistEnabled false
New participant with no prior registration
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for participant on full event
GET registration-status for the participant if a registration row exists, or verify null with rejection audit
cases.expected: Participant can determine rejection outcome — either Rejected state in registration-status or null with registration attempt blocked and reason available via error response; no ambiguous Registered or Waitlisted state

cases.title: registration-status for unknown eventId returns 404
preconditions: Authenticated participant with valid JWT
eventId that does not exist in database
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{nonExistentEventId}/registration-status with Authorization header
cases.expected: HTTP 404 with typed error code; no registration payload returned; safe failure for invalid event scope

cases.title: Both cancelled registrant and promoted waitlist participant see updated status on next query
preconditions: Registered participant A and Waitlisted participant B on same full event
Valid cancellation by A triggers FIFO promotion to B
steps: Complete cancellation by A
GET registration-status for A
GET registration-status for B without delay or manual refresh beyond normal API poll
cases.expected: A sees cancelled terminal state; B sees Registered replacing prior Waitlisted; both affected users see updated statuses per user Flow 2 step 5 near real-time after atomic cancellation plus promotion transaction

cases.title: UI shows status unavailable when registration-status lookup fails
preconditions: Participant authenticated on event discovery
Simulated network or API failure for registration-status fetch
steps: Load event discovery with registration status hook encountering error
Inspect affected event card status area
cases.expected: Card displays Status unavailable or equivalent non-misleading fallback; does not show incorrect Registered or Not registered; user is not presented with stale success state

cases.title: registration-status responds within acceptable latency under normal load
preconditions: Event with moderate registration volume
Authenticated participant with existing registration
Normal load test environment (not peak spike)
steps: Measure elapsed time for GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status
Repeat for GET /api/v1/me/registrations with pageSize 20
cases.expected: p95 response time meets NFR-04 quick response under normal load; participant status queries remain suitable for near real-time UI refresh patterns

cases.title: Status token mapping produces consistent human-readable labels for each registration state
preconditions: Status token or badge mapper module for participant-facing registration states
steps: Map each canonical state (Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn, Attended, Absent, Cancelled variants, Rejected) to display label
Verify same state always maps to same label and semantic tone
cases.expected: Labels are understandable and consistent per NFR-12; no duplicate conflicting labels for the same state; supports event card and detail panel reuse

cases.title: Registration action provides immediate status feedback on success or failure
preconditions: Participant on event detail with registration action available
steps: Submit registration and observe inline status panel or toast before navigating away
Attempt duplicate registration and observe failure feedback
cases.expected: Success shows Registered or Waitlisted immediately with reason when applicable; failure shows explicit reason not generic error; satisfies NFR-13 immediate feedback for critical registration action

cases.title: registration-status snapshot matches latest committed registration row after concurrent writes
preconditions: Event with capacity contention
Multiple concurrent registration or promotion operations using transactional consistency
steps: Complete concurrent registration writes with Idempotency-Key per request
GET registration-status for each participant after all writes settle
cases.expected: Each status read reflects latest known entity state after commit; no stale pre-transaction state; mutating endpoints return latest snapshot per API idempotency contract and NFR-02 consistency

cases.title: registration-status exposes only participant-safe fields
preconditions: Participant authenticated with active registration
Registration row includes internal audit metadata not meant for participant display
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status
Inspect response fields
cases.expected: Response includes registrationId, eventId, participantId, state, updatedAt, and waitlistPosition when applicable; does not leak other participants' data or excessive internal operator metadata per NFR-09

cases.title: getStatus query is side-effect free and returns versioned registration snapshot
preconditions: Registration module query contract: queries are side-effect free
Participant with known registration state in test fixture
steps: Invoke getStatus twice without intervening mutations
Compare both responses
cases.expected: Both calls return identical registration snapshot; no audit writes or state mutations occur; read path suitable for frequent near real-time polling

cases.title: Waitlisted participant status shows Expired when registration window closes without promotion
preconditions: Participant Waitlisted on event
Registration window closes via close-registration or time transition
Participant was not promoted to Registered
steps: Close registration window with participant still Waitlisted
GET registration-status for the waitlisted participant
cases.expected: Status reflects Expired or equivalent terminal waitlist state per registration state machine; participant can track that waitlist entry will not convert after window close
